guide_resume_instruction.context: Stage 1 is COMPLETE and approved. Closure was verified by Guide instance 2 against devplatform commit eaa005f and BlarAI commit 1d06135. This directive authorizes one between-stages cleanup pass before Stage 2 kickoff. Four issues require resolution. All four are well-scoped edits; none re-open Stage 1 work or affect Stage 1 closure status.
guide_resume_instruction.authority: Lead Architect (LA) explicitly authorized using this still-open Executor session for between-stages cleanup. Stage XMLs are normally locked during execution per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml hard_boundaries, but LA approval grants this between-stages window. STATUS.md will record the work as a between-stages cleanup commit with anomaly entries.

item.name: F1 fix -- generalize _project_context.py project_id lookup (Stage 2 XML item 2.2)
item.location: Inside <item id="2.2" name="Create project-root resolution helper">, inside the <file path="C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\_project_context.py"> block, in the resolve() function, the project_id assignment.
item.change:
REPLACE this block: project_id = ( cli_project_id if cli_project_id is not None else registry.get("blarai") or registry.get("project_id") )
WITH this block: # Schema convention: each project's reverse-lookup yaml has a # "<projectname>_project_id" key (e.g., "blarai_project_id" for the # project rooted at C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI). The generic "project_id" # key remains as a backwards-compat fallback for projects that opt # out of the prefixed convention. name_key = f"{root.name.lower()}_project_id" project_id = ( cli_project_id if cli_project_id is not None else registry.get(name_key) or registry.get("project_id") )
item.reason: The yaml schema BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml contains "blarai_project_id: 3" (per Stage 1.5 XML), so registry.get("blarai") returns None and resolve() raises ValueError. Generalizing to a name-derived key works for BlarAI today and for any future project (devplatform_meta, others) without further code changes.

item.name: F2 codification -- add <vikunja_access_protocol> block to Stages 2, 3, 4 XMLs
item.location: Insert immediately AFTER the closing </comprehension_gate> tag in each file, BEFORE the <v2_updates> block. Use identical block content in all three files.
item.block_to_insert: <vikunja_access_protocol added_on="2026-04-24" rationale="Stage 1 Anomaly A5 -- Executor surfaces vary in MCP-tool exposure; REST is documented as functionally equivalent fallback"> <intro> Stage operations that read or write Vikunja MAY use either Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools OR direct Representational State Transfer (REST) against the Vikunja API. Both paths are byte-equivalent at the Vikunja server. Preference: MCP when the Executor surface exposes mcp__vikunja__* tools; REST is the documented fallback when MCP is not surfaced. </intro> <preferred path="MCP"> Tool prefixes: mcp__vikunja__list_projects, mcp__vikunja__create_project, mcp__vikunja__list_tasks, mcp__vikunja__create_task, mcp__vikunja__update_task, mcp__vikunja__add_task_comment, mcp__vikunja__list_labels, mcp__vikunja__add_label_to_task. Precondition: Vikunja v2.3.0 server running on http://localhost:3456 (start via Start-Process C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\vikunja\vikunja-v2.3.0-windows-4.0-amd64.exe; poll /api/v1/info before first call). </preferred> <fallback path="REST"> Direct REST via Invoke-RestMethod. Authentication flow: $cred = Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json $pass = $cred.mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS # read-only; do NOT write back $body = @{ username='blarai'; password=$pass } | ConvertTo-Json $token = (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri 'http://localhost:3456/api/v1/login' -Method POST ` -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json').token $headers = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } # Subsequent calls: GET/PUT/POST against /api/v1/projects, /api/v1/tasks, # /api/v1/labels, etc. </fallback> <credential_source> Single canonical source for Vikunja credentials: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json key path mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS. Both MCP path and REST path read this same value. The User-scoped VIKUNJA_PASS env var (persisted at Stage 0.0.5) is used by the MCP server process itself; for direct PowerShell scripts, read from the .vscode/mcp.json file as shown above. </credential_source> <safety> Neither path satisfies Master Plan section D.1 enforcement -- D.1 is enforced by tools/_vikunja_client.py (created in Stage 2.1) for fleet RUNTIME tools, not for stage-execution operations. Stage operations using either path must still pass explicit project_id when the operation is project-scoped. </safety> </vikunja_access_protocol>
item.reason: Stage 1 EA hit MCP-tool unavailability and successfully fell back to REST (Anomaly A5). Codifying the fallback eliminates A5-style anomalies at every subsequent stage and makes the procedure resilient to Executor-environment variance.

item.name: F3 fix -- correct $BLARAI_PID regex at three preflight sites
item.change: At each of the three locations below, REPLACE this regex pattern: 'blarai:\s*(\d+)' WITH this regex pattern: 'blarai_project_id:\s*(\d+)' The surrounding PowerShell code is unchanged; only the Select-String pattern argument changes.
item.reason: The yaml schema you wrote at Stage 1.5 (per the locked XML's prescription) is "blarai_project_id: 3", so the regex 'blarai:\s*(\d+)' does not match. Without this fix, every Stage 2.8 / 3.7 / 4.7 preflight would throw "BLARAI_PID not found in registry; complete Stage 1.4 first." despite Stage 1 being correctly complete.

item.name: F4 fix -- promote agent_gates_bus to fleet-level in devplatform/projects/registry.yaml
item.change:
REMOVE this line from the blarai_auxiliary_projects: map: blarai_agent_gates: 6
ADD a new entry to the top-level projects: section, AFTER the fleet_reports: entry: agent_gates_bus: vikunja_project_id: 6 note: "Shared gate bus across all platform-managed projects. Labels 9-14 (Gate:Pending-SDO through Gate:Escalation) post against this project. All projects' SDO / Co-Lead / EA gate states route here. Per BlarAI/CLAUDE.md section Vikunja Conventions (Agent Gates bus, project 6)."
item.reason: Project 6 IS the Agent Gates bus per BlarAI/CLAUDE.md -- a fleet-level shared resource referenced by labels 9-14. Misclassifying as "blarai_auxiliary" would mislead future tooling that queries the registry for fleet-level resources. After multi-project, devplatform_meta will route gates here too. The Vikunja-side project name ("BlarAI Agent Gates") stays as-is -- not renaming it server-side is out of scope for this cleanup.

item.name: Q7 follow-on -- promote architecture_decisions to fleet-level in devplatform/projects/registry.yaml
item.change:
REMOVE this line from the blarai_auxiliary_projects: map: architecture_decisions: 5
ADD a new entry to the top-level projects: section, AFTER the agent_gates_bus: entry (which itself is added in C4): architecture_decisions: vikunja_project_id: 5 note: "Cross-project Architecture Decision Record (ADR) tracking. Vikunja project name lacks any project prefix, indicating fleet-level scope. ADR proposals from any platform-managed project route here for review and cataloguing. BlarAI's project-specific ADRs (ADR-010, ADR-011, ADR-012, etc.) remain documented in BlarAI/docs/; this Vikunja project tracks the cross-project ADR workflow. Promoted from blarai_auxiliary_projects per Stage 1 closure review Q7."
item.reason: Vikunja project_id=5 is named "Architecture Decisions" with no project prefix. Per the same naming logic that flagged Project 6 (the Agent Gates bus) as fleet-level, the lack of prefix here is the strongest available signal that this project is intended for cross-project ADR tracking, not BlarAI-specific. After multi-project, devplatform_meta ADR proposals would also route here. Worst case (if Stage 6 investigation reveals it's actually BlarAI-specific), the cost to re-classify is low; meanwhile the fleet-level classification is the more defensible default given the name.

item.name: Q8 follow-on -- annotate remaining blarai_auxiliary_projects with provisional-classification notes
item.change:
After C4 and C6 land, the blarai_auxiliary_projects: map will contain three entries: blarai_infrastructure: 4 blarai_fleet_dashboard: 7 blarai_drafts: 9
REPLACE the entire blarai_auxiliary_projects: block (header + entries) with:
# BlarAI-scoped auxiliary projects. # Provisional classification -- actual usage (active task count, last-update date) # not yet investigated at Stage 1. Stage 6 hardening (per item 6.7.5 follow-on # pattern) should query each via Vikunja API (REST or MCP per vikunja_access_protocol) # and decide one of: # - Keep as auxiliary (active BlarAI work confirmed) # - Archive in Vikunja (no active tasks in 90+ days) # - Reclassify (e.g., promote to fleet-level if the name turned out to be misleading) blarai_auxiliary_projects: # Likely BlarAI Hyper-V VM / OpenVINO / hardware infrastructure tracking. # BlarAI prefix supports project-scoped classification. Verify at Stage 6. blarai_infrastructure: 4 # AMBIGUOUS: name has BlarAI prefix but "Fleet Dashboard" concept is fleet-level. # Could be BlarAI's view of the fleet, OR a fleet dashboard misclassified with the # prefix. Verify at Stage 6 (decide keep / archive / reclassify). blarai_fleet_dashboard: 7 # Likely BlarAI scratchpad/drafts project. BlarAI prefix supports project-scoped # classification. Verify at Stage 6. blarai_drafts: 9
item.reason: Task-count and last-updated data for these three projects is not available at Stage 1 (would require starting Vikunja and calling list_tasks per project, which is out of scope for this between-stages cleanup). Premature classification risks dropping active work. Mature path: keep them in auxiliaries (preserves discoverability), document the provisional status with YAML comments (visible to anyone reading the registry), and defer firm classification to Stage 6 when full investigation is on the agenda anyway. Schema stays as `key: int` -- no structural change for future tooling that reads the auxiliaries map.

item.name: STATUS.md anomaly logging + execution log entry

changes.add_to_section:
Append three new entries at the END of the section (after A3):
### A6 -- Stage 2 XML _project_context.py + preflight regex defects (Stage 1 close + 1) -- LOW (fixed)
- **What was different**: Stage 2 XML item 2.2 inline _project_context.py used `registry.get("blarai")` but Stage 1.5's prescribed yaml schema is `blarai_project_id: 3` (Risk F1 surfaced by Guide instance 2 at Stage 1 closure review). Same key-mismatch defect appears in the regex `'blarai:\s*(\d+)'` used at Stage 2.8 / 3.7 / 4.7 BLARAI_PID preflight (Risk F3). - **What was done**: Between-stages cleanup commit on `chore/platform-extraction` generalized _project_context.py resolve() to derive the lookup key from `root.name` (works for BlarAI today and any future project), and updated all three preflight regexes to `'blarai_project_id:\s*(\d+)'`. No code yet exists in the repo (Stage 2 EA writes _project_context.py at item 2.1); the XML defect would have caused ValueError at first runtime call. - **Why safe**: Edits are to locked planning artifacts (stage XMLs) between stages, with explicit Lead Architect authorization. Stage 1 closure status unchanged. Stage 2 EA will paste the corrected code. - **Follow-up**: None.
### A7 -- <vikunja_access_protocol> codification at Stages 2, 3, 4 (Stage 1 close + 1) -- LOW (procedural amendment)
- **What was different**: Stage 1 EA hit MCP-tool unavailability in the Executor surface and fell back to REST (Anomaly A5). Without codification, every subsequent Vikunja-touching stage would re-discover the same gap and re-file an A5-style anomaly. - **What was done**: Added a `<vikunja_access_protocol>` block (immediately after `</comprehension_gate>`) at Stages 2, 3, 4 documenting MCP as preferred and REST as documented fallback, with credential source named at `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json` (`mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS`). - **Why safe**: Both paths are byte-equivalent server-side. Neither bypasses Master Plan section D.1 enforcement (which is for fleet RUNTIME, not stage execution). - **Follow-up**: None.
### A8 -- agent_gates_bus reclassification (Stage 1 close + 1) -- LOW (registry hygiene)
- **What was different**: Stage 1 EA recorded Vikunja `project_id=6` ("BlarAI Agent Gates") under `blarai_auxiliary_projects` in `devplatform/projects/registry.yaml`. Per BlarAI/CLAUDE.md section Vikunja Conventions, project 6 IS the Agent Gates bus -- a fleet-level shared resource referenced by labels 9-14 -- not a BlarAI auxiliary. - **What was done**: Promoted to top-level `projects.agent_gates_bus` alongside `fleet_reports` in registry.yaml. Vikunja-side project name unchanged. - **Why safe**: Registry classification only. No Vikunja API calls. Future devplatform_meta gate-state postings will land at the correct project. - **Follow-up**: Optional -- Stage 6 hardening could rename the Vikunja project from "BlarAI Agent Gates" to "Agent Gates" via `mcp__vikunja__update_project` for naming hygiene. Not required.
### A9 -- architecture_decisions promotion to fleet-level (Stage 1 close + 1) -- LOW (registry classification)
- **What was different**: Stage 1 EA recorded Vikunja `project_id=5` ("Architecture Decisions") under `blarai_auxiliary_projects` in `devplatform/projects/registry.yaml`. The Vikunja project name has no project prefix (unlike `BlarAI Infrastructure`, `BlarAI Drafts`, etc.), strongly suggesting cross-project / fleet-level intent for ADR (Architecture Decision Record) tracking. - **What was done**: Promoted to top-level `projects.architecture_decisions` alongside `fleet_reports` and `agent_gates_bus` in registry.yaml. Vikunja-side project name unchanged. - **Why safe**: Registry classification only. No Vikunja API calls. If Stage 6 investigation turns up evidence that this project is actually BlarAI-specific, re-classification is a one-line revert. - **Follow-up**: Stage 6 hardening should confirm classification by inspecting actual ADR content in the Vikunja project. Specifically: if all ADR tasks reference BlarAI-internal architecture (Hyper-V, OpenVINO, USE-CASE-XXX), re-demote; if any reference fleet/platform/devplatform_meta, the fleet-level classification stands.
### A10 -- blarai_auxiliary_projects provisional-classification notes (Stage 1 close + 1) -- LOW (registry hygiene)
- **What was different**: After A8 (`agent_gates_bus` promotion) and A9 (`architecture_decisions` promotion), the `blarai_auxiliary_projects:` map has three remaining entries: `blarai_infrastructure: 4`, `blarai_fleet_dashboard: 7`, `blarai_drafts: 9`. Their actual usage (task counts, last-update dates) was not investigated at Stage 1 because that requires starting Vikunja and is out of scope for between-stages cleanup. - **What was done**: Annotated each entry with YAML comments documenting provisional classification basis, ambiguity (notably project 7 "BlarAI Fleet Dashboard"), and a Stage 6 hardening directive to query Vikunja and decide keep/archive/reclassify per project. Schema stays as `key: int` -- no structural change for future tooling reading the auxiliaries map. - **Why safe**: Comments only; no behavioral change. Future tooling reads the same int-valued keys. Premature reclassification or archive without task-count data could drop active work. - **Follow-up**: Stage 6 EA should: (a) start Vikunja temporarily, (b) call `list_tasks` (or REST equivalent per `vikunja_access_protocol`) for each of project 4, 7, 9, (c) decide per project: keep / archive / reclassify, (d) update `registry.yaml`, (e) stop Vikunja again. Optionally create a DevPlatform-Meta investigation ticket per the existing 6.7.5 pattern if deferring further.
changes.append_to_execution_log: [2026-04-24 Stage 1 close + 1] Between-stages cleanup pass (consolidated, Q7/Q8 included). Stage 1 EA, authorized by Lead Architect via Guide instance 2. Edits: _project_context.py generalization (Stage 2 XML 2.2), <vikunja_access_protocol> block (Stages 2, 3, 4), $BLARAI_PID regex (Stages 2.8, 3.7, 4.7), agent_gates_bus reclassification + architecture_decisions promotion + auxiliary-projects annotation (devplatform registry.yaml). Anomalies recorded: A6, A7, A8, A9, A10 (all LOW). Stage 1 closure status: UNCHANGED (still COMPLETE).

commit.message: chore(platform_separation): between-stages cleanup -- Stage 2 XML defect fixes + REST fallback codification + STATUS A6/A7/A8/A9/A10
fix(registry): promote agent_gates_bus + architecture_decisions to fleet-level; annotate remaining auxiliaries pending Stage 6 review

commits.ordering: BlarAI commit first OR devplatform commit first -- order does not matter (different repos, no dependency between the two commits).

cmd.label: C2 vikunja_access_protocol present in all three

verification_required_before_signaling_c.cmd: Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\platform_separation\0[345]_STAGE*.xml | ForEach-Object { [PSCustomObject]@{ File = $_.Name; Hits = (Select-String -Path $_.FullName -Pattern 'vikunja_access_protocol' -SimpleMatch).Count } } # expect 2 hits per file (open + close tag)

cmd.label: C3 new regex present in all three

verification_required_before_signaling_c.cmd: Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\platform_separation\0[345]_STAGE*.xml | Select-String -Pattern "blarai_project_id:\\\\s\*\(\\\\d\+\)" # expect 1 match per file

cmd.label: C3 old regex GONE in all three
C7 auxiliaries map has 3 'Verify at Stage 6' comments
C7 three auxiliary entries still present (4, 7, 9)

verification_required_before_signaling_c.cmd: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git log --oneline -1; cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git log --oneline -1
cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git status --porcelain; cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git status --porcelain # both empty

cmd.label: Fleet still paused, Vikunja still stopped

verification_required_before_signaling_c.cmd: python -c "import json; print(json.load(open('C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json'))['fleet_paused'])"; Get-Process vikunja* -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue # True; empty

out_of_scope.item: Do NOT touch Stage 1 work products (devplatform skeleton, registry contents beyond C4, vikunja_project_ids.yaml, .gitignore, placeholder docs). Stage 1 is closed.
Do NOT edit Stage 0 XML or Stage 1 XML -- those are already executed; their defects (if any) are post-procedure review concerns.

Do NOT edit Stage 5 XML or Stage 6 XML -- F1/F2/F3/F4 do not surface there. (Stage 5 doesn't read $BLARAI_PID; Stage 6 doesn't make Vikunja calls beyond the 6.7.5 ticket creation which can use either MCP or REST per A7.)
Do NOT call mcp__vikunja__update_project to rename "BlarAI Agent Gates" -- that is an optional Stage 6 hygiene task (A8 follow-up), not in scope here.
Do NOT touch state.json. Fleet stays paused for the entire procedure.
Do NOT touch stash@{0}. Same reason as Stage 1 acknowledgment.
Do NOT delete or modify GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_DIRECTIVE.xml or GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_BRIEF.md (this directive and its companion brief). They are audit artifacts.

guide_resume_instruction.stage_complete_report_template: Paste back to Lead Architect: 1. Outputs of all 14 verification commands above. 2. The two new commit hashes (BlarAI side and devplatform side). 3. Any deviation from this directive flagged for STATUS.md (anomaly A11+ if needed). After Lead Architect relays back to me and I verify, the path is clear for Guide-handoff emission and Stage 2 kickoff.
